Web Application Configuration and Deployment

Web Application Configuration and Deployment

When you deploy and configure the web application you need to ensure that the application is securely deployed. To do so, the passwords and security mechanisms provided with Uniface must be changed and secured.

When installing Uniface you are asked to provide a username and password for the Uniface Server. This password, as well as the username and password for a database you have specified in the assignment file, is transmitted in plain text.

Security Mechanism Description Location
Password for the Uniface Server The WRD reads the password from its configuration file, and passes it to the Uniface Router, which uses it to start a Uniface Server process. web.xml
Uniface Router access privileges The Uniface Router must have appropriate access privileges to start the Uniface Server. On Unix systems, root privileges are required to change the user ID.

On Windows systems, lower privileges such as the local system account are sufficient.
Password for the DBMS The Uniface Server reads the database password from a local assignment file. local assignment file
Checksum for non-editable fields (NED) The checksum requires an initialization vector, which is set in an assignment file using the $SERVER_SECRET setting.

Uniface has two other mechanisms to ensure secure deployment:

  • PathScrambler—ensures that user names and passwords located in assignment files and Java Servlet settings files are base64-encoded. Use the PathScrambler to secure database and Uniface Server credentials, as well as the initialization vector used to secure non-editable fields.
  • Security Drivers—used to encode client/server communication (messages and headers) or for SMTP authentication.
Note: The Security Driver cannot be used to encrypt network traffic and is not supported by the WRD.

Guidelines

  • Change the credentials for starting server processes and databases before deploying the application. Do not use the default passwords.
  • Encrypt passwords or other connection strings.
  • Do not put the key for encrypted data on the database server. This prevents bulk decryptions if the key is compromised.
  • Encrypt all data that has to transit the network, that is, data going from the WRD via the Uniface Router to the Uniface Server. Otherwise, the Uniface Server password will be transmitted as clear text from the web server to the Uniface Server.
  • Use TLS rather than TCP connections.

Threats

  • Password Cracking

Related Links
  • Pathscrambler

    The Pathscrambler is command-line utility for encrypting sensitive information and values in assignment files and login strings. These may be used in assignment files, servlet settings, the open ProcScript statement and the /log (login) command line switch. Uniface automatically decrypts any data that has been encrypted with the Pathscrambler.

  • Security Drivers and the Security Driver API
  • Authentication for Web Applications

    Authentication is intended to ensure that users (or computers) accessing the system or application are who they purport to be. Authentication systems range in strength from simple user and password validation to transaction signing.

  • $SERVER_SECRET

    Specifies a unique string to be used as a key in calculating the hash-values used to protect non-editable fields from being modified in a web browser.
